era_context: Shalmaneser V is in the second year of his reign, actively prosecuting campaigns in the Levant. The Kingdom of Israel under Hoshea is increasingly tributary to Assyria. In Greece, the Archaic period continues with the development of the hoplite phalanx and early colonisation. Egypt remains fragmented. The Zhou Dynasty in China is in the Spring and Autumn period.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Greece (no specific events for this year in surviving sources)

events.title: Assyrian campaigns in the Levant continue under Shalmaneser V
events.description: Shalmaneser V continues military operations against rebellious Levantine states. While specific engagements for 728 BCE are not individually documented, this year falls within the broader campaign period (729–722 BCE) in which Assyria consolidates control over the region. Tributary states are pressured to maintain compliance; resistance is met with military force and mass deportation.

sources.name: Assyrian King List and fragmentary royal inscriptions

events.certainty_note: No specific event is documented for 728 BCE itself. This entry reflects the broader military context of Shalmaneser V's reign. The year is assigned by convention within the known campaign period.
cross_references: -722-001 (Fall of Samaria, end of campaign period)

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. However, the sparsity of documentation for 728 BCE specifically reflects a genuine gap in the historical record rather than a lack of activity. Shalmaneser V's reign is less well-documented than his predecessors', and year-by-year reconstruction is often impossible.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 728 BCE is extremely thin. Assyrian records survive in fragmentary form; no contemporary sources from other regions document this specific year. Reconstruction relies on interpolation within known campaign periods. This year exemplifies the challenge of year-by-year history in the ancient world: significant events may have occurred but left no datable trace, or may be recorded only in later compilations with uncertain chronology.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Shalmaneser V's reign and military consolidation
